item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A cool little concept, no doubt. I would even upvote this, but there's just no way the Foundation would allow using it as pets, it's kinda of insane really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww, what happened to the image?

EDIT: nevermind, saw it was deemed non-compliant in the search history

item.title: Re: Photoshopping
item.content:encoded: With no indication this or any other source of this image has been released under Creative Commons, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: lol linked loop snakes was awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An anomaly that turned snake into sn∞ke.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just want to point out that the snake in the picture could never achieve a perfect circle without some portion being upside down like a möbius strip. Is this intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just can't stop thinking about how the one in the photo has a full twist in it, and how it could have possibly gotten that way…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to set one loose on an island to see what happens when it hits a critical mass and you have to resort to nukes to kill them fast enough.

It's a X2 growth rate of matter and kinetic energy, leave it alone and it could significantly effect the weight, orbit, and gravity of the planet. Only stopping until it becomes uninhabitable for the snake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't stop laughing at it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want this NOW! +1

But on other notes, i'm actually more interested how this SCP creates energy for itself, all i can imagine is the stark reactor from iron man inside this thing giving it energy. (cause its round for you non-iron man people, like who hasn't seen that movie?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure what I like more, this comment, or this tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ALRIGHT! Fine! You got me! I fully agree it makes zero sense in-universe for the Foundation to let people keep them as pets. So why did I include it in the article?

Because I want one as a pet.

Now, I'm sure that doesn't improve the article or make up for it or whatever, but I'm also not going to change it. I understand that might be to the determent of this article's rating. But, man, I really want a pet loop snake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They're sooo cute.

Anyways, this is very interesting. The last addendum actually cracked me up. Of course, it would be better if there was more strange events and/or a backstory to it. But it did enough for my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a place on the mainlist for articles like this one. It makes sense that the Foundation would have things like this in their possession, which are weird but don't have universe-shattering implications or whatever. Having the more complicated and larger scope articles interspersed with entries like these improves the holistic reader experience.

That all said, these kinds of articles aren't generally my thing. It's written well, so I would have been happy to neutral-vote, had I not seen the "keep them as pets" addendum.

I do not object, in concept, to the existence of the "thing what does a thing" style article. Things can be weird enough in their own right to be interesting and to stick on the site, it's just harder to do these days. I do, however, fundamentally oppose things where the Foundation does irresponsible or nonsensical things because why not. Handing out things that don't obey the laws of science as we understand them as pets makes absolutely no sense from an in-universe perspective, and it introduces an element of goofiness into the article that's really not doing it any favors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That image is actually quite creepy. It definitely makes the article work for me.

item.title: Re: Ummm
item.content:encoded: Oh, snap! The creative mind goes to work and I change it to "Loop Snakes."

item.title: Re: Ummm
item.content:encoded: Suggestions: "autotrophic reptile"; the latinised, binomial name in the description; or "autotrophic serpent." I can't think of others, but I think the best one would be the latinised name.

item.title: Ummm
item.content:encoded: 1203 is also called Ouroboros. You might wanna change that.

item.title: Re: Photoshopping
item.content:encoded:
Would I! This is like someone asking "I noticed the ice cream cone you have is rather small - I would like to give you a larger one. Would you be okay with that?" Thank you, go right ahead!

This is the source image I used: http://s53.photobucket.com/user/TigerQuoll/media/Reptile/poisonous20snake.jpg.html

But feel free to use any picture that works. I'd like to keep the species the same (Inland taipan), but otherwise, go for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, they're like Keter Cakes in the same way that regular snakes are like Keter Cakes: they reproduce. But they don't really pose even a fraction of the danger.

item.title: Photoshopping
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot, but as mentioned above, the photoshopping's fairly crude (no offence) - I would like to help out by creating a picture for this for you.

Would you be okay with that? If you are, would you want me to use any specific source images, or would you be OK with me finding my own?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Me and my big mouth. Oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. An ouroboros that reproduces via Möbius strip? Sign me up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. It looked fine until I read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't you mean… 8?
<3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, right. I guess I've always imagined that personnel live on-site. Obviously it makes zero sense for them to leave Area-12 and enter a civilian area. I'll make that much clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kooky. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why not? Because when I get home from work and I start feeling numbness and pain I am not thinking about taking care of my pets. I am thinking about the heart attack I am having. I am thinking about where I left the asprin and if the house numbers are clear enough.

The EMT who sees the snake tank white his partner is intubating me is thinking "Is that a real animal? How the hell is that thing still alive?"

The animal control officer who arrives to pick up the snake and press animal cruelty charges is thinking "Is this surgical? Who would do that to an animal?"

The veterinarian filling out the report for the police knows something is very wrong. If the Foundation is lucky and careful they will catch her before she calls up other vets for a consult but probably not before photo documentation is on file at the police department and at least 30 people have called over their coworkers to say 'Hey look at this!'. Getting all those pictures off of instagram is going to be a headache.

Maybe at least add a note saying all snakes must be kept in an office on-site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not a keter cake. They're not invincible. If you end up with too many, just, you know, kill some. Also, they only double every eighteen months, not every day.

I'm not a big fan of the pet element either, because for all the Foundation knows these could just start shooting lightning or something, but I really like the idea of the organism and the tone is great. +1, but I think if you want more people on board, lose the personnel pet angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with MacLeod. It's basically an animal version of the Keter Cakes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, the way I see it, they're actually better than regular pets, as far as the Foundation is concerned. No food necessary, no mess, and no chance of losing track of offspring. If site personnel want pets, why not let them have the most convenient pet in the world?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't even really notice the error until you pointed it out in the discussion. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted because "pets." Seriously? As harmless as they seem to be, they're anomalous lifeforms that produce matter from nothing. If they all consistently grow and reproduce, they're basically serpentine Keter Cakes. Keep five for study, sure, but no pets.

EDIT: I wrote this whilst sleep deprived. Therefore, I read "matter and energy from nothing" and immediately thought "cake." However, I have to acknowledge that this simply isn't the case, because these don't reproduce quickly enough and have no defenses against predators. They have to live as pets and lab animals because they would never survive without people helping them.

Downvote neutralized. Will change to up if it's made clear that only personnel living on site can keep them, and restrictions are made obvious.

Also, and this is just an out-of-the-blue idea, what if this were something Wondertainment were marketing as pets?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted it cause its very cute. But, honestly, keep them as pets?!?

I like the nod to the moebius strip and bisecting it into two connecting loops, too =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is adorable. I want five.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's not, um, ideal. I'll try to get a better picture soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dat photoshopping tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now that's what I call a circulatory system! Am I right, or am I right?
